meaning: adj.华丽而庸俗的,俗气的,娼妓的,适合娼妓的
examples: Of the meretricious splendour I had heard so much there is not a trace.
The perspective just as moral truths, that are presented in their simplicity without the meretricious AIDS of ornament or glitter.
It was a platinum fob chain simple and chaste in design, properly proclaiming its value by substance alone and not by meretricious ornamentation — as all good things should do.
There is a boring Luca Giordano and a meretricious pair of Mel Ramos breasts.
He believed in beauty, recognised it in women and, amid the meretricious, created his share of it.
